title: RFE: More methods to trigger CPP preprocessing
body:
Currently, buildah will preprocess Containerfiles that end in `.in` using `cpp -E`. This behavior has been around a long time and has [a dedicated function](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/3b9dbdfd22480c920ba91fa78dfdb01f883c1839/imagebuildah/build.go#L494-L535) to try to robustly find `cpp` in `$PATH` and there have been a few issues over the years [enhancing its behavior](https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/3816). That function is only called [if the file suffix matches exactly](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/3b9dbdfd22480c920ba91fa78dfdb01f883c1839/imagebuildah/build.go#L166-L172), though.

I've been [(ab)using this feature](https://git.jharmison.dev/james/bootc-image/src/branch/main/Containerfile.in) for a while, as it's pretty easy for [bootc](https://bootc-dev.github.io/) Containerfiles to get very long and I found that splitting it up into multiple `#include` statements helped keep things much better organized.

I was recently working on getting [Konflux](https://konflux-ci.dev/) to build my bootc image when I hit a behavior that breaks this - it [copies the provided Containerfile to a randomly-suffixed temp file for manipulation](https://github.com/konflux-ci/build-definitions/blob/2767e956dd6f4b15ab49ce4d52001abd15ad89e5/task/buildah-oci-ta-min/0.9/buildah-oci-ta-min.yaml#L470-L471). I've [raised the topic over there](https://konflux-ci.slack.com/archives/C09LE7E20FP/p1774467719467549) as I think it probably does need tweaked, since the file extension suffix does specifically matter to buildah in this case.

I also think it would be useful to have other methods to trigger the preprocessing function for buildah, though. Arbitrary file names should be able to be preprocessed in the same way, if a command line argument or environment variable were provided to specify it.

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
